user_query:
Please set up an event-triggered automation that watches my book wishlist notes and builds a small research dossier from public catalog pages.

Scope and trigger:
- Watch the folder "input/" for creation or modification of files whose names match "wishlist_*.md". The workflow should run when such a file is created or modified.
- For this request, use the provided input file "input/wishlist_2024-Notes.md" as the test trigger. When it is first processed (or modified), the automation should run and produce the deliverables below.

Processing requirements:
1) Parse the markdown file to extract book entries from bullet lines. Each book line will be of the form "Title — Author" and may optionally include "(ISBN: <digits>)". Ignore non-bullet lines and notes.
2) For each extracted book, perform a web search engine query to locate the book’s page on Open Library (official domain pattern: openlibrary.org). If a clear Open Library page cannot be found, perform another search to try the Library of Congress catalog (official domain pattern: loc.gov). Do not hardcode or directly construct URLs; find the pages via search queries.
3) Choose the best match by aligning the input title/author (and ISBN when available) with the candidate result. Record the "source" as either "openlibrary" or "loc".
4) Download the matched page’s HTML and save a local raw copy. Then extract from the downloaded page the following fields where available: resolved_title, resolved_authors (semicolon-separated), first_publish_year (or publication year), subjects or topical categories (up to 5; semicolon-separated), description_excerpt (first ~300 characters, without line breaks), and the canonical page URL.
5) If no suitable page is found for a book, include it in the outputs with found=false and leave the resolved fields blank. Do not download or save an HTML file for not-found items.

Deliverables (create these on trigger):
- Save each downloaded raw page under "output/webpages/{slug}.html" where {slug} can be a normalized combination of input title and author.
- Write a CSV summary at "output/metadata/books.csv" with one row per input book and the columns: input_title, input_author, input_isbn, found (true/false), source (openlibrary|loc|""), source_url, resolved_title, resolved_authors, first_publish_year, subjects, description_excerpt, html_sha256. The html_sha256 should be the SHA-256 checksum of the corresponding raw HTML file when found, otherwise empty.
- For each book, write a JSON record at "output/metadata/books/{slug}.json" containing: input_title, input_author, input_isbn, found, source, source_url, search_query_used, downloaded_html_path (if found), html_sha256 (if found), retrieved_at (ISO 8601), and the resolved fields listed above.
- Log the trigger to "output/logs/trigger_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>.json" containing at least: triggered_by (path of the file), timestamp, total_books_detected, found_count, and a list of per-book statuses (slug and found flag).

Additional constraints:
- The automation should only act when a new/modified wishlist_*.md file event is detected; do not hardcode processing on startup without an event.
- Use a general-purpose web search engine for discovery (e.g., querying by ISBN or by "<title> <author> site:openlibrary.org"); do not rely on prebuilt URL patterns. Prefer Open Library results; fall back to Library of Congress only if needed. Ensure the matched page domain is either openlibrary.org or loc.gov.
- Include entries that were not resolved in both the CSV and JSON with found=false; leave extracted fields and html_sha256 blank for those.
- Avoid duplicating rows on repeated triggers for the same unchanged wishlist; if the same book appears multiple times in the same file, de-duplicate by normalized (title, author, isbn).

Inputs and outputs are under the current workspace. Please produce the artifacts exactly at the paths specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
